title: Check-ins
description: A heartbeat or report from a scheduled job or cron monitor. state indicates whether the run succeeded, failed, or reported its status.

check_in_id.description: Stable identifier for the monitored check-in / job.

state.description: Reported state of the run. reporting = the job sent a heartbeat for the scheduled window; missing = the window expired without a heartbeat; paused = the monitor is currently paused.

payload.description: Optional user-supplied body, only present on advanced check-ins. The fields below are the ones Honeybadger surfaces in the UI; any other keys are caller-defined — reference subkeys as payload.<name>::<type> and prefer @preview to inspect a sample before guessing field names.

status.description: Job outcome label. "success" is rendered as a check icon in the UI; anything else is treated as a failure label.

duration.description: How long the job took, in milliseconds.

exit_code.description: Process exit code reported by the job. 0 conventionally means success.

stdout.description: Captured stdout from the job run.

stderr.description: Captured stderr from the job run.
